description: Collapsible hierarchical dependency graph, curved connectors, agent lifecycle toast notifications, CCEM Agent integration

userStories.title: Add scoped agent lifecycle notification categories to AgentRegistry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AgentRegistry to support lifecycle notification categories (agent_spawned, agent_complete, agent_failed, agent_timeout, agent_return) with project/namespace/formation scoping so notifications can be filtered and routed appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: add_notification/1 accepts map with required fields: type (info|success|warning|error), title, message, category (agent|formation|upm|skill), and optional scope fields: project_name, namespace, formation_id, squadron_id, agent_id, story_id
get_notifications/1 accepts optional filter keyword list [:category, :project_name, :namespace, :type] and returns filtered results
New notification struct includes: id, type, title, message, category, project_name, namespace, formation_id, squadron_id, agent_id, story_id, timestamp, read
PubSub broadcast on apm:notifications includes full scoped notification
Backward compatible: existing add_notification calls with just title/message/level still work
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes
mix test passes

userStories.title: Create hierarchical graph data builder module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GraphBuilder module that transforms a flat agent list into a d3-hierarchy compatible tree structure, grouping agents by project > formation > squadron > agent, with collapse state tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: GraphBuilder module created at lib/apm_v4/graph_builder.ex
build_hierarchy/2 accepts agent list and opts (scope: :all_projects | :single_project), returns %{name, children, data} tree
All-projects scope: root > project nodes > formation/namespace nodes > individual agents
Single-project scope: root > formation/namespace nodes > individual agents
Agents without formation group under 'ungrouped' namespace node
Each node includes: id, name, type (project|formation|squadron|agent), status, agent_count (for group nodes), children list
collapse_state/2 returns list of node IDs that should start collapsed (everything except root and first level)
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes
mix test passes with at least 5 new test cases

userStories.title: Add toast notification JS hook and SCADA-themed CSS
userStories.description: As a user, I need a toast notification overlay in the APM dashboard that displays agent lifecycle events with the SCADA industrial aesthetic, auto-dismiss, and stacking support.
acceptanceCriteria: New file assets/js/hooks/toast.js created with LiveView hook
Hook handles 'show_toast' event with: type (info|success|warning|error), title, message, duration (default 5000ms), category, agent_id
Toast renders as glassmorphic card in bottom-right corner matching SCADA palette (#151b28 bg, status-colored left border)
Toasts stack vertically with 8px gap, max 5 visible (oldest auto-dismissed)
Each toast has close button, auto-dismisses after duration
Success type uses #7eef6d, error uses #ff6b5a, warning uses #ffaa44, info uses #5daaff
Toast entry/exit CSS animations (slide-in from right, fade-out)
Hook registered in assets/js/app.js Hooks object
Toast container div added to root layout in layouts.ex
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Refactor dependency graph for collapsible hierarchy with curved connectors
userStories.description: As a user, I need the dependency graph to show only top-level nodes (projects or formations) by default, expanding children recursively on click, with smooth curved connector lines between nodes instead of straight lines.
acceptanceCriteria: dependency_graph.js refactored to use d3.hierarchy() for layout instead of flat force simulation
Initial render shows only top-level nodes (projects in all-projects mode, formations/namespaces in single-project mode)
Clicking a group node expands its children with smooth transition animation (750ms)
Clicking an expanded group node collapses it back
Collapsed group nodes show member count badge and aggregate status color
Connector lines use d3.linkVertical() or d3.link(d3.curveBumpY) producing smooth Bezier curves instead of straight lines
SVG arrowhead markers added to connector endpoints to show dependency direction
Existing SCADA aesthetic preserved: dark theme, glassmorphic cards, dot grid, status colors
Keyboard navigation still works (Enter/Space to expand/collapse, arrows to navigate)
Hook still communicates with LiveView via handleEvent('agents_updated') and pushEvent('select_agent')
New handleEvent('hierarchy_data') accepts tree-structured data from GraphBuilder
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes

userStories.title: Enhance notification API with lifecycle events and scoped queries
userStories.description: As an external tool, I need POST /api/notify to accept scoped lifecycle notifications and GET /api/notifications to support filtering by category, project, namespace, and type.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/notify accepts full scoped notification payload: type, title, message, category, project_name, namespace, formation_id, squadron_id, agent_id, story_id, severity
GET /api/notifications accepts query params: ?category=agent&project=ccem&namespace=web&type=error for filtered results
GET /api/notifications returns newest-first, limited to 100 by default (?limit=N supported)
POST /api/notify broadcasts to apm:notifications PubSub topic
Response includes notification ID for reference
Backward compatible: existing notify calls with just title/message/level still work
Routes updated in router.ex
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes
mix test passes with at least 4 new test cases

userStories.title: Integrate collapsible graph and toast notifications in DashboardLive and AllProjectsLive
userStories.description: As a user, I need DashboardLive and AllProjectsLive to push hierarchical graph data via GraphBuilder and display toast notifications for agent lifecycle events in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardLive calls GraphBuilder.build_hierarchy/2 with :single_project scope and pushes via push_event('hierarchy_data', data)
AllProjectsLive calls GraphBuilder.build_hierarchy/2 with :all_projects scope and pushes via push_event('hierarchy_data', data)
Both LiveViews subscribe to apm:notifications and push_event('show_toast', notification) for lifecycle events
Toast notifications only shown for the active project's events (not cross-project)
Graph data re-pushed when agents_updated PubSub event received
Existing flat agent data still pushed via agents_updated for backward compat
Collapse/expand events from JS (pushEvent toggle_node) handled in LiveView, state tracked in socket assigns
mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes
mix test passes

userStories.title: CCEM Agent notification polling and macOS toast display
userStories.description: As a user, I need the CCEM Agent to poll APM for agent lifecycle notifications and display them as macOS notification center toasts, scoped by project and namespace.
acceptanceCriteria: APMClient.fetchNotifications(since:category:project:) method added, calls GET /api/notifications with filters
NotificationPoller added to EnvironmentMonitor: polls /api/notifications every 5 seconds for new notifications since last check
New notifications displayed via UserNotifications framework (UNUserNotificationCenter) instead of osascript
Notification title format: '[project] agent_type status' (e.g., '[ccem] explorer completed')
Notification body includes: agent name, namespace, duration if available
Notification category identifiers set for grouping: com.ccem.agent.lifecycle, com.ccem.formation.lifecycle
Notifications grouped by project in Notification Center
Only notifications with category 'agent' or 'formation' displayed as toasts (skip 'upm' and 'skill' categories)
HealthStatus.swift updated with Notification model matching API response
Typecheck passes (swift build succeeds)
